source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/250411 SKD 인도 - Blue/SKD india 250530/5.7 Verification & Validation/Usability/A-UFEP-BLU Usability Formative Evaluation Plan_250205.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Plan for formative usability evaluation of Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models through survey-based product usability assessment.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines the formative usability test method, criteria, environment, participant qualifications, risk-related usage scenarios, data collection and analysis methods, and practitioner roles.
keyPoints: Formative evaluation is conducted through a survey on product usability using given scenarios.
Participants must match the user profile and the survey is conducted with 5 participants.
Questionnaire score must be 3 points or higher, and acceptance criteria is greater than 4.1.
Usability evaluation must be re-evaluated after product design changes or customer usability requirements.

concepts.name: Formative usability evaluation
concepts.evidence: This document is intended to perform formative assessments based on a variety of factors including the complexity of the medical device, the potential for hazardous use errors, development schedule, and budget

concepts.name: Survey-based usability assessment
concepts.evidence: Formative evaluation of the product is conducted through a survey on product usability.

concepts.name: Participant qualification
concepts.evidence: Survey Participants should be at least as good as the user profile.

concepts.name: Risk-related usage scenarios
concepts.evidence: Usage Scenarios related to risk factors 위해요인 관련 사용시나리오

concepts.name: Usability data collection methods
concepts.evidence: The usability data collection method should be implemented by combining the following approaches.

concepts.name: Usability re-evaluation triggers
concepts.evidence: The usability evaluation must be re-evaluated in the following cases: Changes in the design of the product; When there are customer requirements related to the usability of the product

evidence.claim: The document is a usability formative evaluation plan.
evidence.evidenceText: 사용적합성 형성 평가 계획서 Usability Formative Evaluation Plan

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer: H&abyz Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The product is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name: 의료영상획득장치 (Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector)

evidence.claim: The document applies to models G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP, and G1717FWP.
evidence.evidenceText: Model Name: G1417CW/G1717CW/G1417FW/G1717FW/G1417CWP/G1717CWP/G1417FWP/G1717FWP

evidence.claim: Revision 0 was issued on 2024.10.05 as the first establishment.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No 0 Issue Date 2024.10.05 Note First Establishment
evidence.pageTarget: Usability Formative Evaluation Plan Revision History

evidence.claim: The formative evaluation is performed to screen out user interface design flaws that could potentially result in hazardous use errors.
evidence.evidenceText: effective method to screen out user interface design flaws that could potentially result in hazardous use errors

evidence.claim: Product formative evaluation is conducted through a survey on product usability.
evidence.evidenceText: Formative evaluation of the product is conducted through a survey on product usability.

evidence.claim: Survey participants receive a user manual and training program before the survey.
evidence.evidenceText: Before the survey, the participants are provided with a user manual and training program.

evidence.claim: The survey is conducted with 5 participants qualified for the user profile.
evidence.evidenceText: The participants must be qualified for the user profile, and the survey is conducted with 5 participants.

evidence.claim: The survey schedule is 2024.10.05 to 2024.10.10.
evidence.evidenceText: Survey Schedule 설문조사 일정: 2024.10.05 ~ 2024.10.10

evidence.claim: Questionnaire results must score 3 points or higher.
evidence.evidenceText: When the results of the questionnaire are converted into a score, the score must be 3 points or higher.

evidence.claim: The acceptance criteria is greater than 4.1.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptance criteria: > 4.1

evidence.claim: Usability evaluation must be re-evaluated after product design changes or customer usability requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: The usability evaluation must be re-evaluated in the following cases: Changes in the design of the product; When there are customer requirements related to the usability of the product

evidence.claim: The test environment specifies lighting 600 Lux to 800 Lux, noise 40 dB to 60 dB, temperature 18 ℃ to 23 ℃, and humidity 50% to 55%.
evidence.evidenceText: Lighting 600 Lux ~800 Lux; Noise 40 dB ~ 60 dB; Temperature 18 ℃~ 23 ℃; Humidity 50 % ~ 55 %

evidence.claim: Participant occupation criteria are doctor or radiologist.
evidence.evidenceText: Occupation 직업: Doctor or Radiologist의사 또는 방사선사

evidence.claim: Participant skills and qualifications require licensed doctor or radiology license.
evidence.evidenceText: Licensed Doctor or Radiology Licensed의사 면허 취득자 또는 방사선 면허 취득자

evidence.claim: Risk-related usage scenarios include checking the user manual, connecting the power cord to the power supplier, powering on the detector, cleaning, and battery recharging and replacing.
evidence.evidenceText: Task: Check the user manual; Connect the Power Cord to the Power Supplier; Power on the Detector; Cleaning; Battery Recharging and Replacing

evidence.claim: Data collection methods include advisory panel review, brainstorming, cognitive walkthrough, contextual inquiry, expert review, FMEA and FTA, focus groups, observation, interviews, surveys, task analysis, usability testing, and workload evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Review by advisory panel; Brainstorming on use scenarios; Cognitive walkthrough; Contextual inquiry; Expert review; Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) and Fault Tree Analysis (FTA); Focus groups; Observation; One-on-one interviews; Surveys; Task analysis; Usability testing; Workload evaluation

evidence.claim: Survey results are analyzed according to the test criteria.
evidence.evidenceText: Analyze the survey results according to the Test criteria outlined in section 2.1.

evidence.claim: The test supervisor manages the usability testing process and reviews test results and issues test reports.
evidence.evidenceText: Test supervisor - Overall management of the usability testing process; Review of test results and issuance of test reports
